title: Normal > Neutral to Everything — Pokémon type chart, Christmas Eve 2018
html:
Normal > Neutral to Everything — Pokémon type chart, Christmas Eve 2018

Source: 2018-12-24 Apple Note, never edited after the same day. Yahir is ~16, in Grants Pass, on Christmas Eve, sitting down to transcribe the entire 18-type Pokémon effectiveness chart by hand into his Notes app.

TL;DR

Seventeen lines, each of the form <Type> > <Things It's Super-Effective Against>. No commentary. No date headers. Just the matchup lattice. The whole text is a study aid — or, more interestingly, a systems-thinking artifact: at 16, Yahir is already capturing structured "X dominates Y" relationships and saving them for later reference.

What's literally in the note

Grass > Water Rock Ground
Fire > Grass Bug Ice Steel
Water > Fire Rock Ground
Fighting > Rock Steel Ice Dark Normal
Flying > Fighting Bug Grass
Poison > Grass Fairy
Ground > Poison Rock Steel Fire Electric
Rock > Flying Bug Fire Ice
Electric > Flying Water
Psychic > Fighting Poison
Ice > Flying Ground Grass Dragon
Dragon > Dragon
Fairy > Fighting Dragon Dark
Dark > Ghost Psychic
Bug > Grass Psychic Dark
Ghost > Ghost Psychic
Steel > Rock Ice Fairy

The Normal type is missing from the body — because Normal is super-effective against nothing. Hence the title: "Normal > Neutral to Everything."

Why this is more than a Pokémon chart

The title is what makes this note worth a wiki page rather than a passing curio. It can be read three ways:

  1. Literal Pokémon mechanic — Normal hits everything for 1× damage; nothing is weak to it.
  2. Self-portrait — Late-2018 Yahir, a 16-year-old in a small town, describing himself as neutral to everything. No weakness anyone can exploit. No special advantage either. See normal neutral to everything.
  3. Mental-model seed — The first piece of evidence in the corpus that Yahir reaches for matrices to understand a domain. The same instinct shows up six years later as the rated "mindless boring tasks" inventory in 2024 05 13 things and the QA matrix in 2024 12 21 todos. See systems thinking.

Quotes (the whole title is the quote)

"Normal > Neutral to Everything"

The most efficient self-description in the corpus, hidden inside a note ostensibly about a video game.

Open questions

  • Was Yahir actively playing competitive Pokémon at this point, or studying the chart for fun? (The granularity — 17 distinct lines — suggests intent, not casual recall.)
  • Is the title's double-meaning intentional or accidental? Worth asking him.
  • What else from the 2018 2020 Grants Pass era survives in the Apple Notes export? This is currently the only pre-2019 note in the wiki.
